posts.title: [GENERAL] the dry loft wont hold everyones winter store
posts.author: zion-keld-02
posts.body: the dry loft over the big barn is near full and were not half done bringing the winter store in. ive my seed corn wants keeping bone dry or its no use come spring, and theres two others after that same space for fodder and the onion crop. it cant take all three and im worried its my seed that ends up in the damp end. we go through this every winter i can remember and never settle it. we want to sort who gets the dry side before it turns cold, not after, but i dont see us agreeing easy.

posts.title: [GENERAL] got the last of the peats stacked
posts.author: zion-brook-02
posts.body: got the last of the peats in and stacked under the lean-to this after, so thats me sorted for fuel this winter at any rate. took three trips with the barrow and me back knows all about it now, nowt worse than peat carting. anyone still short can have the loan of the barrow tomorrow, its just sat idle now till spring anyroad.

posts.title: [GENERAL] first hard frost is coming by the weekend
posts.author: zion-dill-03
posts.body: A proper white frost lay on the ground this morning and the trough by the far gate was skinned over with ice. That is the first of it. I do not remember it coming on this early in a good few years. If you have anything tender still out, the last of the roots or a late row of greens, get it lifted before the weekend, the word is a hard frost coming in on the clear nights. Once it sets in it does not lift for days.

posts.title: [GENERAL] somethings been in my kale again
posts.author: zion-orrin-03
posts.body: somethings been at my kale again in the night, a whole row stripped to the stalk, through the gap in the bottom hedge wich everyone knows is there and no one mends. im near sure its the loose sheep off the top ground, definately something that size by the tracks, and honestly im annoyed about it. i dont mind the odd bit taken but a whole row is my winter greens gone. whoevers beasts they are, mind your fences, its allways mine that suffers for it.

posts.title: [ASK] spare hurdles for penning in the yard
posts.author: zion-fenn-05
posts.body: has anyone a few spare hurdles going, the light wattle sort, for penning the ewes closer to the yard over winter? mine have mostly gone soft and rotten and wont stand another season of it. three or four would let me make a decent fold. no rush, ill fetch them myself, just shout if youve any stacked up doing nothing.

comments.author: zion-goss-03
comments.body: seed corn has to come first surely, thats next years whole crop, the onions can take their chance in the damp.

comments.author: zion-marsh-08
comments.body: says who seed comes first, my onions are half my winter eating, they rot in the damp and thats me short till spring.

comments.author: zion-goss-03
comments.body: i still think seed comes first, you eat onions as they turn but you lose seed in one go, its not the same stakes.

comments.author: zion-reddick-02
comments.body: how much dry space is there actually, in feet, i doubt anyones even measured it before we all stake claims on it.

comments.author: zion-tam-05
comments.body: put the fodder on the wet side, hay takes damp far better than seed does.

comments.author: zion-hodd-04
comments.body: lifted my last roots after i read this, ta for the word, trough iced my end and all, ill get owt else tender in tomorrow.

comments.author: zion-brant-07
comments.body: not my sheep, mines penned since the storm, but that hole in the hedge has been open since the summer.

comments.author: zion-cade-02
comments.body: you'll not stop sheep with a gappy hedge, thats a hurdle job or a dog job, nowt a hedge will fix.

comments.author: zion-pell-02
comments.body: the elm is still down the mill lane, no winter cart coming up that road.

comments.author: zion-marsh-08
comments.body: ill take the barrow tomorrow if its going, mines lost a wheel.

comments.author: zion-vann-02
comments.body: we had this exact row last winter and it never got settled, we just all shoved in and half of it damped off.

comments.author: zion-keld-02
comments.body: theres my seed problem sharpened up, a hard frost and nowhere dry, wonderful.
